long_description:
nfs not responding issue mitigated, for rca work creating inc
Incident source: #warroom-vpc-inc10494336 - Phanindra (IaaS CORE)
close_notes:
(All Timestamps must be in UTC)
### Issue Summary: For  #warroom-vpc-inc10494336 RCA | NFS hung in few different nodes in DAL13 - investigation for CIE INC10494336
SME Findings: 
Reason of Escalation:
Resolution Steps:High Level Update:
- Both switches ngas02.sr01.dal13 and ngas01.sr01.dal13 were reloaded and added back up in Production.
- No NFS hung issue observed since then and no more issues observed with dal3-qz1-sr1-rk363-s28.
- We are in monitoring state and will check every 30 min if more new NFS hung appears,
teams calls is closed now as agreed in call and will monitor offline if need we will reopen the call and reengage Storage and NRE team.

Signature of Issue:agonzaga@dal3-qz1-sr1-rk403-s01:~(mz737)$ cat nfs_log_andre_check | grep -i "vsff" | awk '{print $1,$2, $7}'
dal3-qz1-sr1-rk421-s04: vsff-dal1351b-sec-fz.service.softlayer.com:/cci_root/cci_vol1_ccistordal1351a/479-185ae6ac-012c-4bdd-a7e9-52729478e892 (rw,relatime,sync,vers=3,rsize=65536,wsize=65536,namlen=255,hard,nosharecache,proto=tcp,timeo=600,retrans=10,sec=sys,mountaddr=10.200.143.135,mountvers=3,mountport=635,mountproto=tcp,fsc,local_lock=all,addr=10.200.143.135)
dal3-qz1-sr1-rk421-s04: vsff-dal1351b-sec-fz.service.softlayer.com:/cci_root/virt_vol074_dal1351/0737-4189c469-1b46-4a8f-8440-a279f67d624c (rw,relatime,sync,vers=3,rsize=65536,wsize=65536,namlen=255,hard,nosharecache,proto=tcp,timeo=600,retrans=10,sec=sys,mountaddr=10.200.143.135,mountvers=3,mountport=635,mountproto=tcp,fsc,local_lock=all,addr=10.200.143.135)
Jira/Github/Other Tickets: 
Category/Subcategory:is-vpc 	pod failures 	packet loss
Timeline Summary:-Created this INC to follow up on investigation RCA for CIE INC10494336 
-NRE found stale route in two agregated Arista switches ngas02.sr01.dal13 and ngas01.sr01.dal13
-NRE costed out one by one, reload as suggested by Arista team and costed in back up into production 
-We have not seeing any issue with hung NFS since adding those back up
-We checked every hour and not finding any issue since then 


Resolution Runbook: Not Needed
Caused by Change/Incident: 
Warroom:#warroom-vpc-inc10494336
Meeting URL:
Outage Start:
Outage End:


 Other InformationOther links / facts that should be recorded   
-> This investigation lead to NRE CIe retro INC10505174 NFS server is experiencing timeouts. Upon further investigation by NRE, Net Eng and Arista provider it was established that there was inconsistent route issues seen on the ngas02 and later ngas01. A reload of the device and cost back in resolved the issue.

->This Incident broke SLO due a complicated troubleshooting engaging Several different team, Storage, Netapp, NRE, SDN, Neteng, kube, hostos. We had a CIE that also broke a SLO INC10494336 and investigation RCA also broke SLO. Issue was mitigated as mentioned above.

I suggest we add a 
-Category: Service
-Subcategory: Network Underlay
to better reflect the issue for this. I selected "packet loss" because I think it is close to what we saw with switches but is not exactly a pod failure. Please review and suggest a better category/sub. Thanks
current_status_next_steps:
High Level Update:
Both switches ngas02.sr01.dal13 and ngas01.sr01.dal13 were reloaded and added back up in Production.
No NFS hung issue observed since then and no more issues observed with dal3-qz1-sr1-rk363-s28.
We are in monitoring state and will check every 30 min if more new NFS hung appears,
teams calls is closed now as agreed in call and will monitor offline if need we will reopen the call and reengage Storage and NRE team.
NRE team will follow up to have a parent CIE for this issue and report back to us.
